item.title: Hormuz Recovery Hits a Bumpy Patch After Fresh Attacks on Ships
item.description: The reopening of the crucial waterway faltered after Iran struck two ships.

item.title: Microsoft’s $570 Billion Rout Sets Up Its Worst Month Since 2000
item.description: Microsoft Corp. shares are heading for their worst month since the dot-com era as investors continue to fret about how the software giant will fare in a world marked by artificial intelligence.

item.title: UK Growth Outlook Brightens After US-Iran Truce Lowers Oil Costs
item.description: The fall in global oil prices is “unequivocally good news” for Britain’s economy, Bloomberg Economics said as it lifted growth forecasts for this year.

item.title: Mercuria Signs First Uranium Financing Deal With Malawi Miner
item.description: Trading house Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed its first prepayment agreement with a uranium miner, striking a deal with the owner of an operation in Malawi.

item.title: Burnham Vows Economic Boost by Taking Fiscal Power Out of London
item.description: Andy Burnham has pledged to radically devolve fiscal powers away from Westminster should he succeed in his bid to replace Keir Starmer as prime minister.

item.title: Warsh Joins ECB’s Soul Searching in Sintra
item.description: The ECB’s annual forum will puzzle over feeble growth and ponder the dangers of AI

item.title: Ferrari Luce, New Golf Cart Reflect Apple Design Aesthetic
item.description: Ive, Hoenig reflect their Apple experience in new vehicles

item.title: Global Trade Braces for Another Period of Policy Uncertainty
item.description: Ties between China and the EU are fraying, while the North American free trade deal looks set to undergo reviews

item.title: UK Political Angst Turned Foreigners Into Gilt Sellers in May
item.description: Foreign investors, long an anchor for the UK government bond market, turned net sellers of gilts in May as climbing yields failed to offset concern about the country’s political turbulence.

item.title: Verizon Sees Quarterly Loss of Up to $800 Million Due to BT Deal
item.description: Verizon Communications Inc. projected a second-quarter loss of between $700 million and $800 million after striking a deal with UK’s BT Group Plc to create a joint venture for their international businesses.

item.title: China Pushes All School Levels to Teach AI in Xi’s Tech Drive
item.description: China has announced a formal plan to integrate artificial intelligence across all levels of schooling, aligning its education system with President Xi Jinping’s campaign to dominate advanced technologies.

item.title: Nigerian Government Seeks Advisers for Planned Eurobond Offering
item.description: Nigeria invited expressions of interest from advisers for a planned Eurobond sale, the first since a November issuance drew five times more demand than the amount on offer.

item.title: Germany Wants and Needs AI to Take Human Jobs
item.description: The country’s workforce is aging and retiring.

item.title: Comcast Plans to Spin Off NBCUniversal Into New Media Firm
item.description: Comcast Corp. said it plans to separate its media businesses from its cable-TV and internet operations, spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new publicly traded company in a bid to increase value.

item.title: Risks in Focus After US Stocks’ Wild Start to 2026
item.description: Investors are grappling with a series of risks, from AI to rising interest rates and US elections.

item.title: AI Will Drive a Strong US Earnings Season, Goldman’s Snider Says
item.description: US companies are set for another strong earnings season, driven by the AI investment boom and windfall profits for energy companies, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. strategists.

item.title: Booming Bank Capital Markets Hand Weakest Links Free Pass
item.description: In the market for junior bank capital — where distinctions between strong and weak banks matter most — investors are becoming remarkably indifferent.

item.title: Google Cloud to Offer Specialist AI Models for Science Research
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google will start offering specialist artificial intelligence models from software company SandboxAQ through its cloud service, expanding enterprise and research access to technology designed to accelerate drug discovery, materials science and semiconductor manufacturing.

item.title: ‘Southern Squeeze’ Grips US Cities Once Known for Affordability
item.description: Affluent newcomers are reshaping places like Nashville and Atlanta, boosting local economies but straining the budgets of longtime residents.

item.title: El Niño Threatens to Deepen Crisis for Brazilian Rice Farmers
item.description: A looming El Niño is threatening to deepen a financial crisis for rice farmers in southern Brazil, where years of extreme weather have already sent costs soaring and forced the country’s top producing region to slash future planting plans.

item.title: China Births Two Robot Unicorns as Sector Funding Stays Strong
item.description: Two Chinese robotics startups have been valued at more than $2.9 billion in recent funding rounds, demonstrating steady interest in the sector and boosting the country’s efforts to compete with US firms like Tesla Inc. and Figure AI Inc.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Is Biggest Europe Stocks Bull With New Target
item.description: As European stocks were hammered by the Iran war, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Mislav Matejka predicted the declines would be short-lived. The strategist is now doubling down, becoming the biggest bull among forecasters tracked by Bloomberg.

item.title: AirTrunk Is Said to Be Near Confidential Filing for Biggest Singapore IPO in Years
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-backed data center operator AirTrunk is close to filing confidentially for an initial public offering of a real estate investment trust in Singapore, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Chinese Investors Review Bond Holdings as Ratings Face Scrutiny
item.description: Chinese asset managers are reviewing their bond holdings to identify issuers at risk of rating downgrades after regulators stepped up efforts to curb the over-concentration of AAA ratings, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Spain Lifts 2026 Economic-Growth Forecast Despite Iran War
item.description: Spain will continue to outperform the euro area’s largest economies, with the conflict in Iran expected to have only a limited impact, according to new government forecasts.

item.title: Dubai Stocks Set for Best Quarter in a Year as War Premium Ebbs
item.description: Dubai’s stocks index is set for its best quarter in a year after a truce in the Middle East conflict drew investors back to a market they fled when Iranian missile attacks shattered the city-state’s reputation for stability.

item.title: Goldman Backs India 30-Year Bonds as Iran War Impact Contained
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. recommends buying India’s 30-year government bonds as inflation expectations ease and lower oil prices reduce fiscal risks.

item.title: UK Mortgage Approvals Drop as Rush to Lock in Rates Fades
item.description: UK mortgage approvals dropped to the lowest in over two years in May, as a scramble to lock in loans in anticipation of interest-rate hikes fizzled out.

item.title: Godiva Japan Lenders Said to Agree on Nine-Month Loan Extension
item.description: Lenders to Godiva Japan Inc. have agreed to extend by nine months the maturity of about ¥75 billion ($463 million) in loans to the struggling luxury chocolate maker, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Baidu’s CFO on How It Became a Full-Stack AI Player
item.description: From Internet search to chips, LLMs, clouds, and self-driving cars.
